title: Unexpected result of prepared statement
description:
### What happens?

The following prepared statement triggers an unexpected error:
```
CREATE TABLE t0(c0 VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO t0(c0) VALUES ('0.2');
PREPARE prepare_query AS SELECT ((t0.c0)AND(?)) FROM t0;
EXECUTE prepare_query(false);
```

This is the error message:
```
Conversion Error:
Could not convert string '0.2' to BOOL when casting from source column c0
```

But the equivalent query `SELECT ((t0.c0)AND(false)) FROM t0;` can be executed successfully.

### To Reproduce

See above.

### OS:

ubuntu 22.04

### DuckDB Version:

1.3.1.0

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

ChiZhang

### Affiliation:

Tsinghua University

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(c1 BOOLEAN, c2 VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE t2(c3 INT, c4 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO t2(c3, c4) VALUES (1, 0.1), (2, 0.2), (3, NULL);
SELECT (CAST(t1.c2 AS BOOLEAN) AND t1.c1) FROM t1 WHERE t1.c1 IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Prepared statement with string to BOOL conversion error
